mechanism_narrative: An always-on signal using FEDFUNDS predicts that a flat-to-declining policy-rate trend at a near-zero level compresses equity discount rates and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with near-zero flat-to-declining FEDFUNDS do not deliver higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than other periods, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a high-slack threshold signals weakening household income and earnings risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days after labor deterioration becomes visible.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 6.5% or rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases and the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Joint gains in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicate that consumer demand is supported by employment income, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS and PAYEMS signal does not outperform either single-series signal and produces a 20-day hit rate at or below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST below 900 signals weakening housing investment and tighter household balance-sheet conditions, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-900 triggered windows show average 60-day S&P 500 returns equal to or above unconditional returns, or if negative-return frequency is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: An always-on positive INDPRO level and trend signal predicts that improving industrial output raises operating leverage and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO observations fail to produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than falling INDPRO observations, or if the return spread is not positive in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL acceleration while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% signals an inflation scare under constrained policy, raising expected future tightening and compressing equity multiples over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above its 12-month change while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and low-FEDFUNDS interaction has nonnegative mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or adds no predictive power relative to CPIAUCSL alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with firm RSAFS indicates broad real demand and near-term sales momentum, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and RSAFS 3-month growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and RSAFS expansion signal has a 60-day return hit rate below 50% or if its mean forward return is not above the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A negative PAYEMS shock signals abrupt labor-income weakness and recession-risk repricing, causing investors to cut equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change crosses below zero
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS monthly-decline triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below zero.
